links.title: Open Admin UI (full screen)
links.tooltip: Opens the admin UI in a new tab — recommended for editing rows.

options.content:
### How to edit a service

**The live table below reads directly from Postgres.** Each row's `system_id` is a clickable deep link to the edit form in the admin UI — click it, change any field (URL, blackbox module, lab, type-specific params), Save, and the new value is written to the `monitored_system` table immediately. Prometheus picks up the change within 30 s via HTTP-SD.

**Tip:** for a full-width edit experience click **Open Admin UI (full screen)** in the dashboard menu (top-right).

value.title: Edit ${__data.fields["System ID"]} in the admin UI

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  id            AS "DB ID",
  system_id     AS "System ID",
  display_name  AS "Display Name",
  system_type   AS "Type",
  system_group  AS "Lab",
  url           AS "URL",
  COALESCE(blackbox_module, version_strategy, '—') AS "Module / Strategy",
  is_enable     AS "Enabled",
  to_char(updated_at, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI') AS "Last edited"
FROM monitored_system
ORDER BY system_group, system_type, system_id

options.content:
### Opening the Admin UI

The admin UI runs as a sibling service on **port 9119** (separate origin from Grafana, so it cannot be safely embedded as an iframe here without breaking the Grafana session cookie). Open it in a new tab:

→ **[http://localhost:9119/admin/](http://localhost:9119/admin/)** (local docker-compose)  
→ For K8s installs the URL is wherever you point your `monitor-exporter` ingress.

**Auth:** the admin UI validates the Grafana session cookie — you need to be logged into Grafana in the same browser first. The full-page admin UI exposes:

- `Systems` — CRUD on `monitored_system` rows (every row in the table above)
- `Labs` — CRUD on `lab` rows (drives the `system_group` dropdowns on every dashboard)
- `Datasources` — CRUD on the `datasource` table (synced to Grafana every 60 s by `datasource_sync`)

When you create/edit/delete a row, Prometheus picks up the change via HTTP-SD within ~30 s. No YAML edits, no pod restarts.
